title: A street food I love
summary: Describe one street food dish from a stall you love.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about one street food dish you love. Name the city, the stall, and what is in the food. Use present simple and 'there is / there are'.
hintStarters: There is a small table where…
sampleText: My favorite street food is a halal cart on 53rd Street in New York. The cart opens at lunchtime, and there is always a long queue. The vendor is a friendly man from Egypt. He cooks chicken and rice on a big hot plate. He puts the rice in a paper plate, and then he adds chicken, lettuce, and tomato on top. The white sauce is creamy, and the red sauce is very spicy. I always order chicken and rice with both sauces. There are no tables, so I eat on a small wall near the cart. The food is hot, fresh, and cheap. It costs about ten dollars. Many office workers come here every day. Some tourists take photos of the cart. I like the smell of the spices in the air. I often go on Friday because I have a short week then. The vendor knows my face now, and he smiles when he sees me. This small lunch always makes me happy.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: xe đẩy bán đồ ăn halal

sampleGrammarNotes.title: There is / there are
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' với danh từ số ít và 'there are' với danh từ số nhiều để nói cái gì có ở đâu.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' for singular and 'there are' for plural, as in 'there is always a long queue' and 'there are no tables'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn cho thói quen: 'I always order…', 'I often go on Friday'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for habits, like 'I always order chicken and rice'.

sampleTranslationVi: Món ăn đường phố tôi thích nhất là một xe halal trên đường 53 ở New York. Xe mở vào giờ ăn trưa và luôn có hàng dài người chờ. Người bán là một người đàn ông thân thiện từ Ai Cập. Anh ấy nấu gà và cơm trên một vỉ nóng lớn. Anh ấy cho cơm vào đĩa giấy, rồi thêm gà, xà lách và cà chua lên trên. Sốt trắng béo ngậy, còn sốt đỏ thì rất cay. Tôi luôn gọi gà và cơm với cả hai loại sốt. Không có bàn nên tôi ngồi ăn trên một bức tường nhỏ gần xe. Đồ ăn nóng, tươi và rẻ. Nó tốn khoảng mười đô la. Nhiều nhân viên văn phòng đến đây mỗi ngày. Một vài khách du lịch chụp ảnh chiếc xe. Tôi thích mùi gia vị trong không khí. Tôi thường đi vào thứ Sáu vì tuần đó tôi làm việc ngắn. Người bán giờ đã biết mặt tôi và mỉm cười khi thấy tôi. Bữa trưa nhỏ này luôn khiến tôi vui.

options: There is many people at the cart.
There are a long queue.
There is a long queue every day.
There many tables.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a long queue' is singular, so use 'there is'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match 'is' with singular and 'are' with plural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural about a habit?
options: I am ordering chicken every day.
I always order chicken and rice.
I order always chicken.
I will order chicken always.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'always' comes before the main verb in the present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the position of the adverb of frequency.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option best fits the topic 'street food'?
options: I cook a big dinner in a hotel kitchen.
The vendor cooks chicken on a hot plate.
I buy a new shirt at the mall.
My boss writes long emails.
mcQuestions.explanation: Street food is cooked at small carts and stalls.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a stall or cart context.

options: There are a chair near the cart.
There are no tables at the cart.
There is many sauces.
There are sauce on the plate.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'tables' is plural, so 'there are' is correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the food best?
options: The food is boring and old.
The food is hot, fresh, and cheap.
The food is wet and slow.
The food is sleeping.
mcQuestions.explanation: These adjectives describe street food naturally.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose words a real customer would say.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the correct preposition?
options: The cart is in 53rd Street.
The cart is at 53rd Street.
The cart is on 53rd Street.
The cart is by 53rd Street.
mcQuestions.explanation: We say a place is 'on' a named street.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'The red sauce is very ___.'
options: spicy
tall
quiet
fast
mcQuestions.hint: Think about taste, not size or speed.

options: He cook chicken on the plate.
He cooks chicken on the plate.
He cooking chicken on the plate.
He cooked chicken on the plate every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular adds -s in present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a vendor?
options: The vendor sells things at a stall.
The vendor flies a plane.
The vendor teaches at a school.
The vendor drives a taxi.
mcQuestions.explanation: A vendor is a person who sells at a small stand.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the job to selling food on the street.

options: The food is hot but I eat it because it is good.
The food hot good eat I.
Hot the food, good it.
I eat food food food hot.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' and 'because' link contrast and reason clearly.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for natural linkers like 'but' and 'because'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think people in many cities prefer eating at small street stalls instead of restaurants?
